# Water Vendor / Sachet Water Producer Site — Nigeria-First Research Brief

**Niche ID:** P3-water-vendor-water-vendor-site  
**Vertical slug:** water-vendor  
**Niche family:** standalone  
**Family role:** standalone  
**Research date:** 2026-04-26

## 1. Nigerian Business Reality
Nigeria has one of the world's worst clean water access crises — over 60 million people lack safe drinking water. This created a massive water vendor economy: sachet water ("pure water") production and distribution, bottled water brands, and water tanker delivery services. Over 10,000 NAFDAC-registered sachet water producers operate in Nigeria, with Lagos, Abuja, Port Harcourt, and Kano as the primary markets. Sachet water is sold at ₦150–₦300 per pack of 20 sachets (₦500ml sachets). Bottled water (50cl) wholesale ₦2,500–₦4,000 per carton. Water tankers (5,000–20,000 litres) service estates, construction sites, and rural communities at ₦8,000–₦35,000 per trip.

## 2. Target Audience Profile
**Persona A — Gbenga Adebayo, 34, Lagos (Ikorodu)**: Estate manager for a 200-unit development. Needs reliable weekly water tanker supply. Searches Google for "water tanker Lagos". Books via WhatsApp.
**Persona B — Mrs Kelechi Nwosu, 41, Enugu**: Wholesale distributor of sachet water. Wants to find a new NAFDAC-registered producer to add to her supply chain.
**Persona C — Alhaji Usman Garba, 52, Kano**: Runs a water bottling plant. Wants a website to attract institutional clients (schools, hospitals, hotels).

## 3. Recommended Website Structure
**Home:** Hero (company name, "Pure Water. Reliable Delivery." tagline, WhatsApp CTA), services grid (sachet water, bottled water, tanker delivery — with prices in ₦), NAFDAC badge prominently, service coverage map, trust strip.
**About:** Company history, NAFDAC certification, production capacity, quality processes.
**Services:** Product/service pricing in ₦, delivery areas, minimum orders, bulk pricing.
**Contact:** WhatsApp order CTA, address, NDPR form.

## 4. Content Tone & Voice
Clean, trustworthy, health-conscious. "Pure. Safe. Delivered to You." Primary CTA: "Order Water Now" (WhatsApp). Safety and NAFDAC compliance are the #1 trust message — lead with it.

## 5. Trust Signal Inventory
NAFDAC registration number (MANDATORY — displayed prominently); NESREA compliance; SON mark; production capacity ("50,000 sachets per day"); years in operation; ISO 22000 if applicable; delivery fleet size; client testimonials (estate managers, schools, hospitals); CAC registration.

## 6. Image Direction
1. Nigerian factory workers operating sachet water sealing machine — clean production environment, hairnets and gloves, Abuja or Lagos setting.
2. NAFDAC registration certificate framed on factory wall — close-up, trust-forward.
3. Water tanker truck branded with company logo arriving at a Lagos residential estate gate.
4. Stack of sachet water packs on delivery truck, ready for distribution.
5. Pure water sachets being tested in a quality control lab — safety and compliance focus.
6. Estate manager signing delivery receipt as water tanker empties into overhead tank.
7. Rows of bottled water cartons in a clean warehouse — institutional supply capacity.

## 7. Regulatory Compliance Checklist
NAFDAC registration: MANDATORY for ALL sachet and bottled water producers. Number must be displayed on product and website. NESREA: borehole/water source environmental compliance. SON: mark of quality for bottled water. FCT/State Water Board: tanker operation permits. NDPR for contact forms.

## 8. Nigeria-First Compliance Checklist
| Requirement | Status |
|---|---|
| WhatsApp CTA home + contact | ✅ REQUIRED |
| All prices in ₦ | ✅ REQUIRED |
| Nigerian cities | ✅ Lagos, Abuja, Enugu, Kano |
| NAFDAC trust signal | ✅ MANDATORY — lead with it |
| Paystack / bank transfer / POS | ✅ REQUIRED |
| Mobile-first 375px | ✅ REQUIRED |
| NDPR consent | ✅ REQUIRED |
